title: HXCX-QUALITY-002 Replace full scenario source names with upstream-shaped module names
description: Fix the resume live naming mistake before adding more TUI gates. Production Haxe source must not use full scenario/test-history filenames/type names or local validation-slice architecture terms. Move implementation names to concise upstream-domain names, use upstream Codex modules as the architecture oracle, keep explicit audit detail in Beads/docs/harness names only, and add guards/docs so this style is not repeated.
acceptance_criteria: Source implementation names for the resume picker validation gates have a documented shortening strategy; new production-facing modules avoid full scenario/test-history class names; harness names remain explicit where needed for auditability; generated Rust and existing gates continue to pass; no behavior changes are introduced.
close_reason: Replaced full scenario/test-history production names across resume host/live source with concise package-owned names, added source-name guard over src/codexhx/runtime/tui/resume, documented upstream/generated-Rust layout policy, removed ambiguous provenance wording from active docs/source, and verified format, source-name guard, public precommit, generated Cargo portable/metal, live app-server boundary gate, and ninth-cycle replay generated-Rust gate.

The repository must not be considered public-ready until a full history scan reports nonzero commit coverage or equivalent verified history scanning.

title: Add public-readiness hooks, secret scan, format guard, and CI scaffolding

acceptance_criteria: scripts/hooks/pre-commit and scripts/hooks/install.sh exist and can install core.hooksPath; scripts/security/run-gitleaks.sh supports staged and full scans with a non-empty history sanity check; scripts/lint/hx_format_guard.sh supports check/write modes; package.json exposes hook/security/format/public precommit scripts; GitHub CI includes Haxe formatter/build gates and a gitleaks workflow; README/AGENTS document public-readiness requirements; local scans and relevant gates pass.

close_reason: Completed: added repo hooks, hook installer, gitleaks wrapper, Haxe formatter guard, package scripts, GitHub Actions CI/security workflows, Dependabot, Apache-2.0 license metadata, README/AGENTS public-readiness docs, and verified public:precommit plus generated Rust gates.

close_reason: Closed

title: HXCX-4.9 Port VT100 history and render commit invariants
description: Port the first upstream TUI rendering invariants into typed Haxe: row accumulation, history commit behavior, wrapping, Unicode width, ANSI sanitization, and deterministic VT100/string backend output. This is terminal-output parity before taking ownership of the interactive alternate-screen TUI.
acceptance_criteria: A Haxe row/history/render model covers selected upstream vt100_history, vt100_live_commit, and status_indicator behaviors; fixtures cover long-token wrapping, emoji/CJK width, ANSI spans, cursor restoration, word-wrap boundaries, and live commit behavior; interpreter and haxe.rust-generated Rust gates pass; ratatui/crossterm boundary decisions are documented; no interactive terminal ownership or Cafex adapter code is introduced.
close_reason: Implemented selected pure Haxe VT100/history/render invariants: ANSI sanitization, selected emoji/CJK width, row wrapping, history insertion, cursor restoration, and live-row commit draining. Added upstream-derived fixture and interpreter plus haxe.rust-generated Rust harness. Documented that this is string-backend render parity before full ratatui/crossterm interactive ownership.

title: HXCX-4.8 Adapt upstream TUI story replay fixture
description: Adapt the upstream raw Codex TUI story fixture into codexhx-owned replay evidence. Use ../codex only as a read-only oracle, copy or derive the public fixture with provenance, and normalize non-semantic environment noise before comparing codexhx summaries.
acceptance_criteria: A codexhx-owned fixture derived from upstream oss-story.jsonl exists with provenance; typed Haxe parsers cover the selected meta, app_event, key_event, codex_event, and insert_history subset; timestamps/cwd/model/process/terminal noise are normalized; interpreter and haxe.rust-generated Rust replay summary gates pass; the harness does not depend on private upstream Rust test helpers; no Cafex/Cafetera adapter code is introduced.
close_reason: Added codexhx-owned selected fixture derived from upstream oss-story.jsonl; implemented typed TUI story replay parser for provenance, session, app_event, key_event, codex_event, insert_history, and op records; normalized timestamp/cwd/model/session/event noise into stable replay summaries; added interpreter and haxe.rust-generated Rust harness; filed haxe.rust-ojj for generic non-copy class field assign-op lowering exposed by this slice.

title: HXCX-4.7 Build upstream runtime event bus and app-server client facade
description: Build the first Haxe-owned live-runtime shell modeled from upstream AppEvent, AppServerClient, and AppServerSession. This is raw upstream Codex foundation work: typed runtime events/commands, request correlation, lossless vs best-effort event classification, bounded-queue/lag semantics, and an in-memory app-server facade over admitted protocol methods. Keep DTO/state portable where possible and use metal only for Rust-native async/channel ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Typed CodexRuntimeEvent/CodexRuntimeCommand envelopes exist; lossless transcript/completion notification classification matches upstream intent; best-effort drops produce deterministic lag evidence; request/response correlation and typed error categories are fixture-tested; Haxe interpreter and haxe.rust-generated Rust gates pass for the slice; no Cafex/Cafetera adapter code is introduced.
close_reason: Implemented typed runtime command/event envelopes, upstream lossless-vs-best-effort notification classification, bounded event queue lag/backpressure semantics, and an in-memory app-server client facade with request correlation and typed error outcomes. Added fixture-backed interpreter and haxe.rust-generated Rust harness. Filed generic haxe.rust-362 for nullable Array.shift class-return lowering exposed by this slice.

title: HXCX-7.x Upstream or replace haxe.rust CallStack exceptionToString patch
description: The codex-hxrust G1 check profiles require a local haxe.rust working-tree patch in ../haxe.rust/std/haxe/CallStack.cross.hx. The target-local CallStack.exceptionToString previously used Exception directly, causing generated haxe_call_stack_call_stack_impl_.rs to reference an unresolved root Exception type for this external scaffold. Track upstreaming, replacing, or otherwise eliminating this local patch before treating the haxe.rust pin as clean.
acceptance_criteria: Local patch is either merged upstream, replaced by a cleaner haxe.rust fix, or explicitly carried with tests; reference/haxe-rust-local-patches.v1.json is updated; portable and metal check profiles still pass without untracked compiler drift.
notes: haxe.rust fix landed and pushed as 0849fcf1a556cb86615cfcdf635165ba82fec8da. codex-hxrust pin updated by experiments/codex-hxrust/scripts/update-haxe-rust-pin.sh; local patch ledger now marks the Dynamic CallStack workaround resolved-upstream. Added lix scoped library metadata so local codex-hxrust builds can resolve ../haxe.rust without global haxelib dev, while keeping haxelib smoke validation for released consumer behavior.
close_reason: Resolved by haxe.rust 0849fcf1a556cb86615cfcdf635165ba82fec8da. The local CallStack Dynamic workaround is gone, the pin is updated, patch ledger marks it resolved-upstream, and portable/metal generated Cargo gates pass.

title: HXCX-QUALITY-001 Replace manual diagnostic summary walls with structured derived diagnostics
description: Refactor the repeated hand-built key=value summary() walls in report/diagnostic DTOs into a reusable typed diagnostic helper or macro-derived serializer. Keep stable textual evidence available for interpreter/generated-Rust harness assertions, but make typed fields, structured reports, and machine-readable diagnostics the canonical model. Avoid changing runtime behavior or weakening existing parity gates.
acceptance_criteria: Report DTOs used by deterministic TUI/app-server harnesses can emit stable diagnostics without long hand-written string-concatenation walls; existing interpreter and haxe.rust-generated Rust harnesses keep passing; generated Rust remains readable; diagnostics are clearly boundary/debug evidence rather than primary source models; AGENTS guidance remains reflected in implementation.

Initial pass added codexhx.runtime.diagnostics.DiagnosticSummary/DiagnosticField and migrated HXCX-TUI-209 scheduled render execution report summary away from a hand-built plaintext concatenation wall while preserving harness-compatible rendered diagnostics. Continue sweeping older report DTOs in batches.
HXCX-TUI-229 exposed that deep replay-chain report.summary() calls can become expensive and produce oversized generated-run output. The local gate now uses compact boundary diagnostics, but the broader quality slice should derive reusable bounded/depth-aware diagnostic summaries instead of hand-written nested summary walls.

title: Track haxe.rust metal/no-hxrt Rust-native wall-clock time facade
description: Add or plan a generic haxe.rust typed native facade for Rust wall-clock time APIs in metal/no-hxrt code. Current rust.* time surface exposes rust.Instant/rust.Duration helpers for monotonic timing, but not SystemTime/UNIX_EPOCH or chrono-like wall-clock/calendar APIs. The goal is not to change portable Haxe Date semantics or add Codex-specific behavior; it is to let Rust-first Haxe code use typed Rust-native wall-clock primitives without app-side raw __rust__ and without requiring hxrt when the selected contract is metal + rust_no_hxrt.
acceptance_criteria: A future haxe.rust change exposes a typed, product-neutral API such as rust.SystemTime/SystemTimeTools with no app-side raw __rust__; it works in metal and rust_no_hxrt where applicable; portable Date remains hxrt-backed for Haxe semantics; docs clarify when to use Haxe Date vs Rust-native time; tests or snapshots prove generated Rust uses std::time APIs and does not introduce Codex-specific assumptions.
close_reason: Implemented in haxe.rust 6d544cf506e668bc56b66c7d2cbe0abf5094667e with direct rust.SystemTime externs, no-hxrt coverage, rust_path_time snapshot coverage, and codex-hxrust pin validation.

title: Track generic haxe.rust HxString reuse across ternary fallback move lowering
description: HXCX-TUI-158 exposed a portable generated-Rust move/borrow mismatch when a rejected-branch ternary fallback reused a previously moved `HxString` local for a fallback value and later borrowed the original local for preservation evidence. This must be reduced to a product-neutral haxe.rust fixture and fixed generically in the compiler/runtime, not as Codex-specific source or compiler behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal non-Codex Haxe/haxe.rust fixture demonstrates reused non-Copy string fallback lowering without borrow-after-move in generated Rust; the fix lands in `../haxe.rust` under that repo's AGENTS.md/testing rules, generated Rust remains idiomatic and warning-clean, codexhx gates continue to pass without Codex-specific compiler hooks, and the known-good haxe.rust pin is updated only after the compiler change is committed and pushed.
close_reason: Fixed generically in haxe.rust commit 5fdc545bfc44b9e50a2c5dcf2249ac5f5f95f5db. Added string_ternary_fallback_reuse snapshot fixture, remaining-read branch fallback cloning, loop read accounting, and closure-captured reusable-local guard; refreshed snapshots. Validated haxe.rust targeted snapshots, full snapshot refresh, check-metal-policy, local-path/deprecated-define guards, and codex-hxrust generated portable/metal Cargo gates via pin update plus npm run public:precommit.

title: Track haxe.rust class-typed field-record default initialization pressure
description: While adding the codexhx resume picker app-server event-pump boundary, generated Rust eagerly initialized a class-typed field-record default for ResumePickerAppServerEventPumpDispatch.hostEvent before assigning the provided field. That called ResumePickerHostEvent.new(Default::default()) and panicked in hxrt anon access during cargo run. codexhx worked around the issue with a nullable dispatch hostEvent, but haxe.rust should get a generic minimal fixture and lowering fix so class-typed field-record defaults do not construct invalid placeholder objects unnecessarily.
acceptance_criteria: A generic haxe.rust fixture covers a class with a field-record constructor containing a class-typed field and a provided value; generated Rust must not construct an invalid nested default object before assignment, cargo run must pass, and the fix must remain independent of Codex/codexhx naming or behavior.
close_reason: Fixed generically in haxe.rust commit 104e05430ce48d8ed0444ebf05235deaba1d1111. Added class_field_record_default_reuse snapshot fixture and constructor lowering now uses null/reference-safe placeholders for emitted class-reference fields assigned by the constructor instead of eagerly invoking nested field-record defaults. Validated haxe.rust targeted snapshots, full snapshot sweep, check-metal-policy, local-path/deprecated-define guards, codex-hxrust generated portable/metal Cargo gates via pin update, and npm run public:precommit.

title: Track haxe.rust nullable JSON enum match lowering pressure

Keep any fix generic in ../haxe.rust, not Codex-specific.
acceptance_criteria: A product-neutral haxe.rust fixture covers nullable haxe.json.Value optional lookup plus switch/match lowering; generated Rust compiles or the intended authoring pattern is documented; codexhx can revisit the local typed lookup workaround.
close_reason: Fixed upstream in haxe.rust 8b7b97b24f19577dda522e7d0cae33853a4ff44c / haxe.rust-qsoq with product-neutral json_nullable_value_switch snapshot; codexhx pin updated after generated Cargo gates and resume picker kernel validation.
